title: malus × dawsoniana
intro: Malus × dawsoniana, also known as the Chinese crabapple, is a hybrid plant species derived from the cross between Malus baccata and Malus prunifolia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its use in traditional Chinese medicine to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Malus × dawsoniana include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate fever, and aid in digestion, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Malus × dawsoniana is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and its bark and leaves can also be used to make herbal preparations such as tinctures and decoctions.
